LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA AND NEWSPAPER REPORTED DAMAGE FROM A THUNDERSTORMS OVER SOUTHERN EDMOND. FOUR POWER POLES WERE DOWNED AND FOUR OTHERS DAMAGED AT THE EDMOND HYUNDAI AUTOMOBILE DEALERSHIP. THE DOWNED POLES RESULTED IN AT LEAST 8 VEHICLES BEING DAMAGED.

LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA AND NEWSPAPER REPORTED MINOR WIND DAMAGE AT THE DEVON TOWER IN OKLAHOMA CITY. A 5 FOOT BY 10 FOOT PIECE OF GLASS WAS BROKEN ON THE NORTEHAST SIDE OF THE DEVON TOWER WHEN A CRANE WHEN THE WINDS BLEW A CRANE INTO IT.
THE WIND CAUSED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO AN ABANDONED BUILDING.

570 PEOPLE WERE EVACUATED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING OF HONEY CREEK. SEVERAL OF THOSE WERE UNABLE TO RETRIEVE THEIR VEHICLES DUE HIGH WATER OVER A LOW WATER CROSSING... BUT WERE EXPECTED TO BE ABLE TO DO SO MONDAY. THE TIME OF THE FLASH FLOODING IS APPROXIMATE.

A BRIEF TORNADO OCCURRED NORTH OF SHARON AND MOVED SOUTHEAST. NO DAMAGE OCCURRED WITH THE TORNADO.

MULTIPLE STORM CHASERS, LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA, AND THE WOODWARD COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO EAST OF SHARON. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST OF SHARON AND MOVED SOUTHEAST, DESTROYING A 20 BY 40 FOOT METAL SHED AND DETACHING A ROOF FROM A HOME. THE TORNADO LASTED FOR 3 TO 4 MINUTES. THE TIME AND LOCATION ARE APPROXIMATE BASED ON RADAR.
THE BACK WINDSHIELD OF A CAR WAS SHATTERED.

*** 20 INJ *** THIS TORNADO BEGAN WEST OF THE SOUTH CANADIAN RIVER... OR ABOUT 3.7 MILES SW OF NORMAN. THE TORNADO ENTERED THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF NORMAN AT 1601 AND CROSSED I-35 A SHORT TIME LATER. EF-1 DAMAGE OCCURRED IN TWO PLACES... ONE WAS ON EITHER SIDE OF THE INTERSTATE... AND THE OTHER WAS BETWEEN LAHOMA AND PETERS AVENUES NORTH OF MAIN STREET. THE TORNADO FINALLY LIFTED NEAR NE 12TH AND ROBINSON STREET AT 1612 CDT.
MULTIPLE SIGHTNINGS OF A TORNADO IN NORMAN. MULTIPLE REPORTS OF DAMAGE AND POSSIBLE INJURIES.

THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND LIFTED APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES WEST-NORTHWEST OF CHEROKEE. TOWARD THE END OF ITS LIFE CYCLE... A SECOND TORNADO DEVELOPED 2 MILES EAST.
THIS TORNADO OCCURRED SIMULTANEOUSLY WITH A TORNADO THAT WAS LOCATED TWO MILES WEST. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND LIFTED NEAR BYRON AND AMORITA. A CEMETARY WAS DAMAGED JUST WEST OF BYRON.
THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND CROSSED INTO KANSAS APPROXIMATELY 2 WEST OF MANCHESTER IN GRANT COUNTY AT 2039 CDT.

*** 6 FATAL, 29 INJ *** THE TORNADO BEGIN AROUND 2350 CDT 3 MILES NORTHEAST OF ARNETT AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO WOODWARD COUNTY. THE TORNADO MOVED INTO THE SOUTHWEST SIDES OF THE CITY LIMITS OF WOODWARD AROUND 0018 CDT, AND EXITED ON THE NORTH-NORTHWEST SIDE OF TOWN AT 0023 CDT. THE ENDING TIME FOR THE TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 0027 CDT 4 MILES NORTH OF WOODWARD.

KFOR-TV STORM CHASER REPORTED A BRIEF TORNADO 2 MILES WEST OF NASH. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED WITH THE TORNADO.

KFOR-TV AND ALFALFA COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A TORNADO THAT FORMED ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE GREAT SALT PLAINS LAKE. THE TORNADO DAMAGED AN OUTBUILDING ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE LAKE BEFORE MOVING NORTH. THE TORNADO LIKELY LIFTED SIX MILES NORTH-NORTHWEST OF JET AROUND 1955 CDT.

LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA AND A STORM CHASER OBSERVED A TORNADO THAT BEGAN ABOUT 4 MILES NORTH-NORTHWEST OF NASH. IT IS SOMEWHAT UNCERTAIN AT THIS TIME WHETHER THIS WAS A SINGLE TORNADO OR IF MORE THAN ONE TORNADO OCCURRED. THE TORNADIC CIRCULATION BEGAN TO OCCLUDE 8 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF MEDFORD, AND BEGAN MOVING MORE NORTH. THE TORNADO APPEARED TO HAVE LIFTED A MILE OR SO SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF WAKITA.

THIS TORNADO DEVELOPED SOUTHWEST OF MEDFORD AND MOVED NORTHEAST. THE MESONET SITE 1 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MEDFORD REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 84 MPH AT 2110 CDT. THE TORNADO LIKELY MOVED JUST SOUTH OF THE SITE, AS WELL AS SOUTH OF THE TOWN OF MEDFORD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST, OR EAST-NORTHEAST, MISSING THE TOWN OF DEER CREEK TO THE NORTH. DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO HUNDREDS OF POWER POLES AND THE HOHMANN SUBSTATION WEST OF DEER CREEK. THE TORNADO LIKLEY LIFTED A COUPLE OF MILES EAST OF DEER CREEK.
THE WIND GUST OCCURRED AS A LIKELY TORNADO MOVED SOUTH OF THE SITE.

THIS TORNADO DEVELOPED BETWEEN 3 AND 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF NASH. THE TORNADO MOVED EAST-SOUTHEAST TOWARD POND CREEK, LIFTING JUST TO THE NORTH OF TOWN AROUND 2150 CDT. MINOR DAMAGE WAS REPORTED NEAR POND CREEK, WITH SEVERAL LARGE TREES UPROOTED.
